anti_claims: This skill JSON seed does not flip source authority away from the current skill markdown projection.
Resolved operated-standard and acted-on-kind edges are structural route mappings, not proof the skill is complete or used correctly by agents.
Absent mechanism or concept neighbours are residual pressure, not evidence that no such neighbours exist.
Generated graph, health, and atlas projections cannot be read back as source evidence.

omitted: mechanism or concept neighbours not named by typed source mapping
omission_receipt.reason: Skill instance preserves public-safe markdown source identity, digest, and typed route mapping only. JSON parity does not prove full skill-body completeness or runtime correctness.

residual_pressure.reentry_condition: Bind skill.uses mechanism and skill.applies concept edges only when source evidence names ids.

justification.summary: This markdown binds paper-module authoring to std_microcosm_paper_module, the paper_module doctrine kind, and executable doctrine grammar because paper modules must be admitted through capsule/source-coupling evidence and generated projection boundaries rather than broad prose.

title: Microcosm Paper Module Author
triggers: A paper module needs governed admission through JSON capsule/source-coupling evidence rather than remaining legacy Markdown projection.

The agent can name the subject organ or mechanism, capsule/source refs, source digest or residual gap, code loci, doctrine refs, omission receipt, authority ceiling, and generated projection boundary.
workflow: Read std_microcosm_paper_module, core/paper_module_capsules.json, generated paper-module JSON, legacy Markdown boundary, relation registry, and generated health before editing.
Admit only source-backed capsule rows or typed residual pressure; do not mint doctrine, organs, mechanisms, or subject ids merely to unblock Mermaid or Atlas projections.
Regenerate the paper-module corpus and doctrine lattice, then run paper-module, standard, skill, and broad projection checks before scoped commit.
